title: Problem Validation
description: Ensure the startup solves a real and significant customer problem before building the product.

title: Customer Discovery
description: Conduct interviews and surveys to understand customer pain points and expectations.

title: Target Audience
description: Clearly identify the primary and secondary customer segments most likely to use the product.

title: Market Size
description: Estimate the Total Addressable Market (TAM), Serviceable Available Market (SAM) and Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM).

title: Competitor Research
description: Analyze existing competitors, identify strengths, weaknesses and market gaps.

title: Unique Value Proposition
description: Clearly define what differentiates the startup from competitors.

title: Pricing Strategy
description: Choose an appropriate pricing model such as subscription, freemium, one-time purchase or commission.

title: Go-To-Market Strategy
description: Define marketing channels, customer acquisition strategy and product launch plan.

title: Revenue Model
description: Identify how the startup will generate sustainable revenue and achieve profitability.

title: Scalability
description: Evaluate whether the business can expand across regions, industries and customer segments.

title: Customer Retention
description: Develop strategies to improve customer satisfaction, engagement and long-term retention.

title: Risk Assessment
description: Identify technical, operational, financial and market risks before launching the startup.
